content.full_text:
#1

RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

March 24, 1997


Richard Bain
United Professional Firefighters
Political Action Committee
43447 Calle Carabana
Temecula, CA 92592

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-97-88

Dear Mr. Bain:

This is in response to your request for advice under the campaign reporting provisions of
the Political Reform Act.¹ Since you are merely seeking information on your ability to amend
your committee's statement of organization, your letter is considered a request for informal
assistance.²

QUESTION

Can the United Professional Firefighters Political Action Committee amend its statement
of organization to reflect a newly-formed employees' union which will be the new sponsor of the
committee?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Whenever any information on a committee's statement of organization has changed,
including the name of the sponsor, the committee must amend the statement of organization to
reflect the addition, deletion or modification.


¹Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless
otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Section 18000-18995.
All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

²Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. I-97-88
Page 2

FACTS

The United Professional Firefighters is a 73-member labor union local in Los Angeles
County which has historically sponsored its committee, the United Professional Firefighters
Political Action Committee, through member payroll dues. Half of the members of the existing
union will be forming a completely separate union with a different name and different officers.
This newly-formed union will assume the existing political action committee and become its
sponsor under the name of the new union.

You indicated to me by telephone that the remaining members of the United Professional
Firefighters will not be forming a new general purpose committee.

ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act requires every committee to file with the Secretary of State a
statement of organization within 10 days after it has qualified as a committee. The committee
shall file the original of the statement of organization with the Secretary of State and shall also
file a copy with the local filing officer, if any, with whom the committee is required to file the
originals of its campaign reports. (Section 84101.)

A statement of organization contains such things as the name, street address and
telephone number, if any, of the committee. If the committee is a sponsored committee, the
name of the committee shall include the name of its sponsor on its statement of organization.
The statement of organization also contains the full name, street address and telephone number
of the treasurer and other principal officers of the committee. (Section 84102.) Whenever there
is a change in any of the information contained in a statement of organization, an amendment
shall be filed within 10 days to reflect the change. The committee shall file the original of the
amendment with the Secretary of State and shall also file a copy of the amendment with the local
filing officer, if any, with whom the committee is required to file originals of its campaign
reports pursuant to Section 84215. (Section 84103.)

A "sponsored" committee is a committee, other than a candidate controlled committee,
which has one or more sponsors. Any person, except a candidate or other individual, may
sponsor a committee. A person sponsors a committee if any of the following apply:

(1) The committee receives 80 percent or more of its contributions from
the person or its members, officers, employees, or shareholders.

(2) The person collects contributions for the committee by use of payroll
deductions or dues from its members, officers, or employees.

(3) The person alone or in combination with other organizations, provides
all or nearly all of the administrative services for the committee.

File No. I-97-88
Page 3

(4) The person, alone or in combination with other organizations, sets the
policies for soliciting contributions or making expenditures of committee funds.

(Section 82048.7.)

In your letter, you state that the union's political action committee historically receives all
of its contributions through member payroll deductions. The committee is thus "sponsored" by
the firefighters' union and the union has properly incorporated its name into the name of the
committee.

According to your facts, the 73-member union is now splitting into two separate
firefighters' unions and the separating union will assume the responsibilities of maintaining the
committee. The new union will have a new name, as well as new officers.

The committee can effectuate all of these changes by amending the statement of
organization currently on file with the Secretary of State. The amendment must reflect the new
name of the committee, the name of the new treasurer and names of responsible officers, if any.
The new name of the sponsor must also be amended on the statement of organization, and as you
correctly pointed out, the name of the committee must include the name of the newly-formed
union as its sponsor.

In your letter you asked if it was not possible for the new union to assume the committee,
how would you proceed with termination and would you need to return contributions to the
members? Since the new union can assume the responsibilities of the existing committee by
amending the statement of organization, it is not necessary to answer these additional questions.

I have enclosed a Form 410, Statement of Organization, for your use in amending the
statement of organization. The original amended Form 410 must be filed with the Secretary of
State, and if the committee's only activity is within Los Angeles County, then a copy of the
amended Form 410 must be filed with the Los Angeles County clerk as well. If you need any
assistance with this form, or have additional questions, please feel free to give me a call at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

File No. 1-97-88
